summary: Both prior BLOCKING R2 holes are closed and the revision introduces no new issues. (1) CWD-SAFETY MANDATED: R2 requirement step (4) and the dedicated 'R2 (cwd-safety mandate)' Design Decision now MANDATE os.Chdir to the RESOLVED MAIN REPO ROOT (explicitly 'NOT the bead worktree') immediately after WorktreeOps.Remove and BEFORE any post-removal bd/state op. Correctly grounded in the complete.go pattern — verified internal/complete/complete.go:576 carries the unconditional os.Chdir(root) after worktree removal (spec-092 Req 3c), and the spec cites that precedent accurately. Chdir-to-resolved-root (not the deleted worktree) is the right target. (2) ORDERING FIXED: order is now resolve-root+path -> dirty-check (refuse unless --force) -> Remove FIRST -> Chdir -> set-open LAST -> rewind cursor. The recorded 'R2 (ordering)' rationale correctly contrasts the rejected bead->open-first order (which leaves an open+re-claimable bead pointing at a stale worktree, with re-claim worktree-path collision) against the chosen remove-first/set-open-last order, whose partial-failure state is the recoverable 'still-claimed, worktree-gone' (re-run release or `mindspec next` recovers). Dirty-check is correctly sequenced BEFORE removal and refuses unless --force; --force is correctly specced as a mindspec-level pre-gate (not a bd passthrough) because bead.WorktreeRemove is hardcoded --force at the bd-CLI layer (verified internal/bead/bdcli.go:202-203). (3) CWD AC ADEQUATE: the new 'R2 (cwd-safety, spec-092 Req 3c)' AC requires a REAL temp-git executor (newRepoExecutor-style, explicitly NOT fake WorktreeOps), cwd set INSIDE the bead worktree, and asserts post-removal cwd recovery + a subsequent post-removal bd op succeeds. This pins hole 1 where the fake-WorktreeOps AC structurally cannot (the AC itself states why: the fake never deletes the dir). Recipe is real and proven — internal/executor/executor_test.go:112 newRepoExecutor + TestCompleteBead_WorktreeRemoveRunsFromRepoRoot (L333) are the exact precedent the AC mirrors. (4) R1 PRECEDENCE RESOLVED: the 'Positional vs --pick precedence' Design Decision now mandates error-on-conflict when both positional and --pick are given. (5) NO NEW ISSUES: the bd-state-mutation reconcile Design Decision additionally closes the prior secondary concern (MINDSPEC_ALLOW_MAIN / issues.jsonl re-export reconciliation) by mirroring complete/next. Design-sound; clear to approve.
